description_long: This sculpture depicts a seated male figure draped in classical robes, with a contemplative expression and a beard, reminiscent of ancient Greek philosophers. The figure is positioned on a rectangular plinth, with one arm resting on his lap and the other holding a scroll or cloth. The base of the sculpture features an inscription in Greek letters, suggesting a specific identity, likely Aristotle. The back of the sculpture shows the folds of the robe cascading over the chair, and the chair itself has a simple, blocky design. The material appears to be a light-colored stone or marble, with subtle surface texturing to mimic the look of carved stone. The overall form is compact and solid, with fine details in the facial features, hair, and drapery.
description: A detailed stone-like sculpture of a seated, bearded man in classical robes, inscribed with Greek letters on the base. The figure is lifelike, with intricate drapery and a contemplative pose.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full face, detailed beard, draped robes, and Greek inscription on the base.
description_view_1: Left side view highlights the profile of the face, the arm resting on the lap, and the layered folds of the robe.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the back of the chair, the drapery flowing over it, and the back of the head and shoulders.
description_view_3: Right side view reveals the other profile, the hand holding a scroll or cloth, and the side of the chair.
attribute_location_context: No specific installation context is visible; the background is neutral and does not indicate an environment.
